channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How a fight over proxy voting for new parents upended the US House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Anna Paulina Luna was at her Florida home in fall 2023, caring for her newborn son and turning over a question in her mind as a member of Congress: “How do I change this?” Luna, then a first-time mom and first-term lawmaker, could no longer fly to Washington to cast votes in
item.dc:creator: LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump proclaims himself 'in good shape,' but the results of his physical aren't immediately released
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump had an annual physical Friday and concluded, “I did well,” praising his own heart, soul and cognitive ability while noting medical reports from White House doctors may not be ready until the weekend. The 78-year-old, who in January became the oldest in U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Market volatility complicates work for lawmakers in Alaska, a state whose fortunes are tied to oil
item.description: President Donald Trump's on-again, off-again tariffs and a related drop in oil prices have contributed to the uncertainty for Alaska lawmakers tasked each year with crafting a state budget based on volatile oil prices.
item.dc:creator: Becky Bohrer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump admin. 'engaging' with order on Salvadoran prisoner: Official
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court this week said the government must facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge rules
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be forced out of the country as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana ruled Friday after lawyers argued the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump reaches deals with 5 law firms, allowing them to avoid prospect of punishing executive orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced deals Friday with five law firms that will allow them to avoid the prospect of punishing executive orders and require them to together provide hundreds of millions of dollars' worth of free legal services for causes his administration says it suppor
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump opens window for a deal with Iran but issues warning if things don't work out
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is betting that a beleaguered Iran is so vulnerable following a tumultuous 18 months in the Middle East that it might finally be ready to abandon its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Senate Minority Leader Johnson is optimistic lawmakers will make session deadline
item.description: State lawmakers have a big hill to climb with just over a month before the Legislative session is over. Despite economic uncertainty, Senate Minority Leader Mark Johnson (R-East Grand Forks) said he’s optimistic they’ll get a budget passed before the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Lukas Levin, and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: WATCH: Dems spar with whistleblower who exposed children's hospital for performing trans surgeries on minors
item.description: Whistleblower Dr. Eithan Haim faced tough questions from House Democrats after he leaked medical documents exposing transgender medical procedures at a Texas children's hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania man charged with threatening Trump, ICE agents, other officials
item.description: A Pennsylvania man has been federally charged for threatening to assassinate President Trump and other officials, the Department of Justice said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House responds to Supreme Court ruling on mistaken deportation
item.description: The White House on Friday responded to the Supreme Court’s ruling that the Trump administration must “facilitate” the return of a Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rather than "effectuate" the return.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Trump nominee Caine for chairman of Joint Chiefs of Staff in overnight vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan “Razin” Caine to become the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff on Friday, filling the position almost two months after President Donald Trump fired his predecessor. Trump nominated Caine to become the top U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge blocks Trump admin from revoking temporary legal status of hundreds of thousands of migrants
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday moved to block the Trump administration from taking away the temporary legal status of immigrants given to them by the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colorado Democratic senator announces run for governor
item.description: Democratic Sen. Michael Bennet, who has served in the Senate for more than 16 years, announced a Colorado gubernatorial bid Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trump administration offers no details on mistakenly deported man after court ruling
item.description: A federal judge lambasted a government lawyer who couldn’t explain what, if anything, President Donald Trump’s administration has done to arrange for the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge allows requirement that everyone in the US illegally must register to move forward
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday allowed the Trump administration to move forward with a requirement that everyone in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: President Trump directed agencies that regulate energy and the environment to sunset a wide array of environmental protections in an executive order issued Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota Department of Health workers rally against Trump administration cuts
item.description: Members of the Minnesota Association of Professional Employees rallied outside a state office building on the grounds of the State Capitol Thursday to speak out against cuts to public health programs.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: The order applies to all regulations issued under laws governing things like energy appliance standards, mining and offshore drilling — as well as the Endangered Species Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: International students are seeing their visas revoked for infractions as minor as traffic violations.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Giant bust of Elon Musk's head vandalized near Starbase in Texas
item.description: "We don't want this to happen again because it was an effort to put it up and very expensive," the owner said.
item.dc:creator: Mia Morales, NewsNation

item.title: New York lawmakers push 'Peanut's Law' to reform wildlife enforcement
item.description: The death of P'Nut, an Instagram-famous squirrel, inspired legislation aimed at reforming wildlife enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Trump tariff drama
item.description: Financial markets soared on Wednesday after President Trump issued a 90-day pause in almost all the tariffs that had roiled the global economy during the past week.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: As measles spreads, federal budget cuts force closure of vaccine clinics
item.description: Federal funding cuts, though temporarily blocked by a judge, have upended vaccination outreach across the country, including in Arizona, Minnesota, Nevada, Texas, and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Bram Sable-Smith, Arielle Zionts, and Jackie Fortiér, MPR News

item.title: Public bathroom access in NYC to be voted on by City Council
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Knowing where the closest bathroom is in New York City can be a challenge, but a new City Council bill may change that. Councilmember Sandy Nurse is leading the push to expand public bathroom access.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Exclusive: Pence agrees Trump’s tariff policy a potential midterm liability for GOP
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration’s aggressive use of tariffs poses a potential risk for Republicans in the midterm elections, even as he expressed relief about a walk-back of some of the most severe tariffs on allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: U of M president outlines efforts to combat antisemitism in wake of White House investigation
item.description: University of Minnesota President Rebecca Cunningham sent a letter to faculty, students and staff underscoring her commitment to preventing antisemitism. A key regional Jewish advocacy organization says the school is making the right moves.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Cortez Masto proposes tax relief for working class Americans
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto and other colleagues on Wednesday introduced a new bill in Washington, D.C., aimed at putting more money back in the pockets of working Americans. Democratic Nevada Sen.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Real ID rules take effect in May; Minnesota officials say it’s time to apply
item.description: Starting May 7, travelers will need a Real ID or approved alternative to get through airport security. Officials say about 40 percent of Minnesotans have a Real ID. They’re encouraging everyone to get the necessary travel documents ahead of the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration plans to strip NYC of $188M for shelter program: Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The Trump administration is planning to strip New York City of $188 million, Mayor Eric Adams announced on Tuesday. The $188 million includes more than $80 million that the Federal Emergency Management Agency clawed back from the city in February.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Jeffries accuses Trump of tanking the economy to create opportunities for the rich
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Monday accused President Trump of intentionally crashing the economy to benefit the GOP’s wealthy donors and other well-heeled interests.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal workers in Nevada jobs? Democrat's legislation opens arms to Trump cast-offs
item.description: The federal government is shrinking, and that might be an opportunity for Nevada state government to find some experienced new employees.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House next week
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC
